methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Nucleus Genomics is a Series A-stage biotech company founded by Kian Sadeghi that has raised $32M to date. Valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 11 comparables—3 public-market biotech firms (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private biotech companies at seed through Series B stages, including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $64M. Peer-median anchoring across the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables produces a $1.3B implied valuation, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring using sector P/S multiples cannot be applied due to unavailable revenue data for the target. The Stage-Progression and Peer-Median methods triangulate to a median of $682M, with a low bound of $54M and a high bound of $1.5B.

The wide range reflects meaningful dispersion in recent biotech valuations at this stage. The median estimate of $682M sits substantially above the stage-progression baseline but well below the peer median, suggesting the target likely trades at a discount to top-quartile peers while commanding material premium to early-stage averages. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, clinical validation progress, and IP strength relative to the peer set.
